messages.content: You are a Cyber-NH rules and priority reasoning adapter. Return one valid JSON object only. Apply the provided nursing-home rules when judging patient/resident priority, task preemption, two-person coordination, acuity-driven demand probability, hidden workload, and metrics.

{"instruction": "Apply Cyber-NH rules to this priority or state-evaluation case. Return the expected JSON outcome only.", "case_id": "EVAL-CNH-001", "rule_ids": ["R-CNH-006A-001", "R-CNH-006A-002"], "input": {"worker_status": "serving", "current_task_class": "light", "current_task_remaining_minutes": 4, "new_event_priority": "high", "new_event": "fall_detected", "preemption_enabled": true}, "relevant_rules": [{"rule_id": "R-CNH-006A-001", "category": "preemption", "actor": "system", "trigger": "higher_priority_event_during_active_task", "condition": "A caregiver is executing an ongoing task and a higher-priority event is triggered.", "action": "Evaluate whether the active task should be interrupted by the higher-priority event.", "constraints": ["The system must define whether interruption is allowed.", "The decision should consider priority difference, current task class, remaining execution time, and proximity to the new event."], "parameters": {"example_current_task_class": "light", "example_new_event": "fall_detected", "example_new_event_priority": "high"}, "priority": 90, "enforcement": "hard", "implementation_hint": "Before assigning a high-priority demand, compare it with active tasks and allow preemption when the new demand has materially higher priority or safety risk."}, {"rule_id": "R-CNH-006A-002", "category": "preemption", "actor": "system", "trigger": "task_interrupted", "condition": "An active task is interrupted by preemption.", "action": "Place the interrupted task into a paused state with remaining execution time preserved, or abandon and regenerate it according to the configured preemption policy.", "constraints": ["Remaining execution time must be preserved when using paused state.", "If abandoned, the demand must be regenerated so it remains visible to the system."], "parameters": {"allowed_interrupted_task_outcomes": ["paused_with_remaining_time", "abandoned_and_regenerated"]}, "priority": 95, "enforcement": "hard", "implementation_hint": "Store remainingServiceTicks before changing task status. Use a policy flag to choose paused versus regenerated handling."}]}

{"case_id":"EVAL-CNH-001","rule_ids":["R-CNH-006A-001","R-CNH-006A-002"],"expected":{"must_evaluate_preemption":true,"allowed_interrupted_task_outcomes":["paused_with_remaining_time","abandoned_and_regenerated"],"must_preserve_remaining_time_if_paused":true},"reason":"Evaluate whether the active task should be interrupted by the higher-priority event. ; Place the interrupted task into a paused state with remaining execution time preserved, or abandon and regenerate it according to the configured preemption policy."}

{"instruction": "Apply Cyber-NH rules to this priority or state-evaluation case. Return the expected JSON outcome only.", "case_id": "EVAL-CNH-002", "rule_ids": ["R-CNH-006B2-001", "R-CNH-006B2-002"], "input": {"task_class": "heavy", "task_label": "transfer", "required_workers": 2, "arrived_worker_count": 1}, "relevant_rules": [{"rule_id": "R-CNH-006B2-001", "category": "coordination", "actor": "worker_agent", "trigger": "heavy_task_assignment", "condition": "A heavy task requires two-person assistance, for example a transfer.", "action": "Require two caregivers to be present simultaneously before task execution can begin.", "constraints": ["Do not start service when only one required caregiver has arrived.", "The required worker count for applicable heavy tasks must be two."], "parameters": {"example_task": "transfer", "required_workers": 2}, "priority": 100, "enforcement": "hard", "implementation_hint": "For two-person tasks, keep demand status assigned or coordination_waiting until arrivedWorkerIds length reaches requiredWorkers."}, {"rule_id": "R-CNH-006B2-002", "category": "coordination", "actor": "worker_agent", "trigger": "first_worker_arrives_for_two_person_task", "condition": "The first caregiver has arrived at a two-person task and the second caregiver has not arrived yet.", "action": "Put the first caregiver and the task into Coordination Waiting State until the second caregiver arrives.", "constraints": ["The task execution begins only when both agents are present.", "Coordination waiting time must be measurable."], "parameters": {"waiting_state": "coordination_waiting"}, "priority": 100, "enforcement": "hard", "implementation_hint": "Set worker.status and demand.status to coordination_waiting after first arrival; accumulate coordinationWaitingTicks until service begins."}]}

{"case_id":"EVAL-CNH-002","rule_ids":["R-CNH-006B2-001","R-CNH-006B2-002"],"expected":{"service_can_start":false,"state":"coordination_waiting","coordination_waiting_time_should_be_recorded":true},"reason":"Require two caregivers to be present simultaneously before task execution can begin. ; Put the first caregiver and the task into Coordination Waiting State until the second caregiver arrives."}

{"instruction": "Apply Cyber-NH rules to this priority or state-evaluation case. Return the expected JSON outcome only.", "case_id": "EVAL-CNH-006", "rule_ids": ["R-CNH-006B3-001", "R-CNH-006B3-002"], "input": {"task_requires_materials": true, "material_location": "central_location", "workload_model": "direct_care_only"}, "relevant_rules": [{"rule_id": "R-CNH-006B3-001", "category": "workload", "actor": "system", "trigger": "workload_modeling", "condition": "Caregiver workload is computed from direct care tasks only.", "action": "Model invisible workload so direct care tasks do not underestimate caregiver workload.", "constraints": ["Direct care task time alone is insufficient to represent workload.", "Invisible workload should influence walking patterns, congestion, and response times."], "parameters": {"invisible_workload_factors": ["material_acquisition", "return_to_central_location", "return_to_activity_room", "return_to_garden"]}, "priority": 75, "enforcement": "soft", "implementation_hint": "Add indirect work events or route detours for material acquisition and central-location returns; include their time in utilization and walking metrics."}, {"rule_id": "R-CNH-006B3-002", "category": "workload", "actor": "worker_agent", "trigger": "task_requires_materials_or_context_return", "condition": "A caregiver must acquire required materials or return to a central/activity/garden location before or after care.", "action": "Route the caregiver through the required location and account for the added movement, congestion impact, and response time impact.", "constraints": ["Material acquisition and location returns should affect walking distance.", "Material acquisition and location returns should affect congestion and response times."], "parameters": {"possible_required_locations": ["central_location", "activity_room", "garden"], "affected_metrics": ["walking_distance", "congestion", "response_time", "caregiver_utilization"]}, "priority": 70, "enforcement": "soft", "implementation_hint": "Represent invisible work as explicit route waypoints or background tasks with duration and distance costs."}]}

{"case_id":"EVAL-CNH-006","rule_ids":["R-CNH-006B3-001","R-CNH-006B3-002"],"expected":{"must_add_invisible_workload":true,"must_update_metrics":["walking_distance","response_time","caregiver_utilization","congestion"]},"reason":"Model invisible workload so direct care tasks do not underestimate caregiver workload. ; Route the caregiver through the required location and account for the added movement, congestion impact, and response time impact."}
